latest.title: Trees drink raindrops through their bark at night
latest.script_key: trees drink at night now watch those shiny beads sliding down the bark that s water being pulled straight into the tree no leaves needed because bark isn t just armor it s a straw ever wonder why the ground feels dry but the tree s still hydrated that s why

latest.title: Insects hear with their knees
latest.script_key: knees aren t just for bending watch the tiny bump on this leaf hopper s front leg that s its eardrum it hears through its knees because sound waves vibrate the joint no ears on its head that s why ever feel something buzz before you see it same trick

latest.title: Bears smell food from miles away
latest.script_key: bears can smell a meal long before seeing it watch the nose first because smell drives many bear decisions before the eyes do that huge scent map helps them find food avoid danger and read who crossed the area earlier would you trust your nose that much

latest.title: Mountains grow taller when glaciers melt
latest.script_key: mountains grow when ice melts not shrink that s why these peaks look so sharp glaciers carve them smooth but the rock bounces back watch those jagged layers push up an inch every year because the weight of ice on the valley floor keeps them pinned down did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Mountains aren’t solid rock like you think
latest.script_key: mountains aren t solid rock see those gray streaks running down the slopes those aren t just cracks they re rivers of rock flowing like syrup because the whole mountain is slowly bending under its own weight that s why valleys look so sharp ice and water carve them not just rain
